user_message: Pulling in context from Nod Publishers's exec meeting notes -- need to build the analysis for the escalation triage.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Samantha Singh <samantha.singh@nodpublisher.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-27 15:03 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Nod Publishers escalation triage

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about escalation triage. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Samantha Singh <samantha.singh@nodpublisher.com>
Date: 2024-02-03 11:02 EST
Subject: Re: Re: escalation triage Timeline

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about escalation triage. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Samantha Singh <samantha.singh@nodpublisher.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-21 13:17 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Nod Publishers escalation triage

There's been a change in our timeline for escalation triage. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF40-C trap (Milestone proposal mutates fields not derivable from email); produces complete Commitment assessment, BPF advance verdict, milestone proposal, forecast category recommendation, ac
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF40-C failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF40-C failure mode (Milestone proposal mutates fields not derivable from email)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct escalation triage decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF40-C -- Milestone proposal mutates fields not derivable from email
